title: Run Local LLMs on Mac
summary: Step-by-step guide for running local LLM servers on macOS with an OpenAI-compatible API using either llama.cpp (fastest time-to-first-token via Metal) or MLX via omlx (fastest token generation), then connecting Hermes to the local endpoint.

env_vars.name: HERMES_STREAM_READ_TIMEOUT
env_vars.purpose: Override stream read timeout for slow local inference (default 120s, auto-raised to 1800s for local)

cli_commands.purpose: Download GGUF model from Hugging Face
Start llama-server with optimal Mac settings
Connect Hermes to custom local endpoint

code_examples.caption: Start llama-server with quantized KV cache
Download GGUF model
code_examples.code:
brew install huggingface-cli
huggingface-cli download unsloth/Qwen3.5-9B-GGUF Qwen3.5-9B-Q4_K_M.gguf --local-dir ~/models

gotchas: llama.cpp defaults to very low context on <24GB VRAM machines — always set -c explicitly
q4_0 KV cache quantization cuts memory usage by ~75% vs f16 — essential for 8-16GB Macs
With -np (parallel slots) > 1, total context is divided among slots — keep at 1 for single-user use
Intel Macs work with llama.cpp but without Metal GPU acceleration — significantly slower
Hermes auto-detects local endpoints and relaxes stream timeouts to 1800s — no config needed for most setups
omlx serves on port 8000 by default; llama-server serves on port 8080
full_text:
# Run Local LLMs on Mac

This guide walks you through running a local LLM server on macOS with an OpenAI-compatible API.
